title: ScrollArea Component
content:
The `ScrollArea` component provides a customizable and accessible scrollable container, built using Radix UI's `ScrollArea` primitive. ## Purpose This component allows you to create scrollable areas within your application, enhancing user experience for content that exceeds the visible viewport. It offers flexibility in styling and behavior through its props and the accompanying `ScrollBar` component. ## Key Exports ### `ScrollArea` This is the main component that wraps the scrollable content. It accepts standard React props and `ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root` props. **Props:** * `className`: Additional CSS classes to apply to the root scroll area element. * `children`: The content to be displayed within the scrollable area. * `...props`: Any other valid props accepted by `@radix-ui/react-scroll-area`'s `Root` component. ### `ScrollBar` A component used to render the scrollbar within the `ScrollArea`. It's automatically included and managed by the `ScrollArea` component. **Props:** * `className`: Additional CSS classes to apply to the scrollbar element. * `orientation`: Determines the orientation of the scrollbar. Can be `'vertical'` (default) or `'horizontal'`. * `...props`: Any other valid props accepted by `@radix-ui/react-scroll-area`'s `ScrollAreaScrollbar` component. ## Usage Example ```tsx import { ScrollArea } from '@/components/ui/scroll-area'; function MyComponent() { return (

Tags

{Array.from({ length: 50 }).map((_, i, arr) => (

{i + 1}. Tag {i + 1}

))}
); } export default MyComponent; ``` This example demonstrates how to use the `ScrollArea` component to wrap content that might overflow its container, providing a smooth scrolling experience. The `ScrollBar` is automatically rendered to indicate the scrollable position.
